# PACK&GO Refactor Audit — 2026-05-18

**Triggered by:** Vibe Coding workflow Stage 1 (CLAUDE.md §九)
**Scope:** Full repo, P0+P1 only, code-quality (no deps/security)
**Commit at audit:** 4ef11ca
**Generated by:** packgo-code-reviewer subagent

## Executive Summary

The codebase has reached the threshold where shit-mountain risk is real: **507 source files**, **75 over 500 LOC**, **19 over 1,000 LOC**, **40 active tsc errors**, and **zero tests on every payment / agent orchestration / autonomous-agent file**. The single largest risk is `server/routers.ts` (10,122 lines / 293 procedures) — it is operationally a god-file that future AI agents cannot safely modify without breaking unrelated routes. Right behind it: `server/_core/stripeWebhook.ts` (1,019 lines of money path with no tests and only manual ad-hoc idempotency), the entire `server/agents/autonomous/*` subtree (15 files, 5,000+ LOC, 0 tests, multiple files exceed CLAUDE.md size limits), and `server/agents/masterAgent.ts` (3,300 lines orchestrating the AI tour-generation pipeline with no tests). The repo also carries a 102-file `scripts/` graveyard of one-off `.mjs` files that solve recurring problems and should have been promoted to skills or migrations. Top refactor priority should be: split routers.ts → cover stripeWebhook with tests + transactions → fix the 40 tsc errors → split the autonomous agents → purge dead code → consolidate scripts/.

---

## P0 — Must-Fix

### P0-1 · `server/routers.ts` is a 10,122-line god-file
**File(s):** `server/routers.ts`
**LOC:** 10,122 (33× the CLAUDE.md §九 hard limit of 300)
**Severity reason:** This is the single biggest maintainability blocker in the repo. 293 procedures (58 public, 44 protected, 198 admin) all live in one file across 19 top-level domain keys (auth, membership, packpoint, vouchers, photos, ai, favorites, browsingHistory, tours, bookings, departures, inquiries, newsletter, admin, imageLibrary, homepage, skills, ...). Multiple pre-existing tsc errors (lines 2453, 2454, 2508, 2532, 4190, 5219, 5912, 5927, 7902). Zero tests. CLAUDE.md §3.2 explicitly says "超過 150 行時拆分到 server/routers/" — this file is 67× past that threshold. Any AI agent editing this file is operating blind; cross-domain merge conflicts are inevitable; new contributors cannot reason about it; the existing `server/routers/*` sub-files (agentRouter, plaidRouter, suppliersRouter, toolsRouter, tourMonitorRouter) prove the pattern works — it just wasn't applied to the rest.
**Recommendation:** Split into `server/routers/{auth,membership,packpoint,vouchers,photos,ai,favorites,browsingHistory,tours,bookings,departures,inquiries,newsletter,admin,imageLibrary,homepage,skills}.ts`. The 19 top-level keys map 1:1 to files. Top-level `server/routers.ts` reduces to a 30-line composition file. Each sub-router gets its own `.test.ts`. Stage 2 design.md must define which procedures cross-call each other so we don't accidentally create circular imports.
**Est. effort:** 12–16 hours (mechanical extraction + 1 test file per router + verify tsc clean after each split)

### P0-2 · `server/_core/stripeWebhook.ts` — money path, zero tests, no DB transactions
**File(s):** `server/_core/stripeWebhook.ts`
**LOC:** 1,019
**Severity reason:** This file handles every payment event (charge.succeeded, payment_intent.succeeded, payment_intent.failed, charge.refunded, customer.subscription.*, visa payments). It performs 20+ separate `db.update` / `db.insert` calls across handlers (lines 190, 218, 429, 437, 492, 568, 586, 668, 674, 682, 768, 804, 834...) with NO `db.transaction()` wrapping multi-step writes. A webhook crash between the booking-update and the packpoint-award (lines 218 / 260) leaves data in a partial state. Idempotency is "manually enforced" with `if (existing) return` checks (commented at lines 176, 227, 260, 484, 747, 929) but there's no central `webhook_events` dedupe table — every handler implements its own pattern, so adding a new event handler is a foot-gun. **Zero Vitest coverage** despite this being explicitly required by CLAUDE.md §七 ("業務邏輯 (AI 生成、支付、搜尋、多語言)"). Active tsc error at line 824. This is the highest-risk file in the codebase: bugs here cost real money.
**Recommendation:** (a) Wrap every multi-write handler in `db.transaction(...)`. (b) Add a `stripeWebhookEvents` Drizzle table keyed on `event.id` and do a single up-front idempotency check, removing the per-handler checks. (c) Write Vitest tests for the 6 main handler paths (succeed/fail/refund for booking + visa) with mocked Stripe events. (d) Fix the type error on line 824 (`"emergency"` not in inquiry-category union). (e) Split into `stripeWebhook/{bookings,visa,subscriptions,refunds,index}.ts` if it remains over 300 LOC after the cleanup.
**Est. effort:** 8–10 hours

### P0-3 · 40 pre-existing tsc errors block "tsc must pass" rule
**File(s):** Across 11 files: `client/src/components/admin/AutonomousAgentsTab.tsx`, `client/src/components/admin/ToursTab.tsx`, `server/_core/requireAdmin.ts`, `server/_core/stripeWebhook.ts`, `server/agents/autonomous/agentTools.ts`, `server/agents/autonomous/opsActions.ts`, `server/agents/autonomous/opsAgent.ts`, `server/agents/autonomous/selfRetrospective.ts`, `server/agents/calibrationAgent.ts`, `server/agents/contentAnalyzerAgent.ts`, `server/marketingWorker.ts`, `server/routers.ts`, `server/services/lionTravelApiService.ts`, `server/services/supplierSyncService.ts`, `server/services/tourMapGenerator.ts`, `server/services/yearEndExportService.ts`
**LOC:** N/A (40 errors)
**Severity reason:** CLAUDE.md §九 explicitly says "tsc --noEmit mandatory" — and "Commit without running tsc" is on the red-lines list. Today the codebase ships with 40 type errors, several of which are **schema drift** (e.g., `agentTools.ts:291,297,299,301,302` reference `bookings.status/adults/contactEmail/contactName` properties that don't exist; `opsAgent.ts:273` references `tours.days` which doesn't exist; `opsActions.ts:316` references `bookings.notes` which doesn't exist; `routers.ts:2453` calls `db.listTours` which doesn't exist; `routers.ts:4190` accesses `tour.priceUsd` which doesn't exist). These aren't cosmetic — they indicate code paths that **would crash at runtime if executed**. The `--downlevelIteration` errors (calibrationAgent, selfRetrospective, lionTravelApiService, yearEndExportService) are a tsconfig issue and should be one-line fixed by raising target/lib.
**Recommendation:** (a) Fix tsconfig target/downlevelIteration first (single config change closes ~10 errors). (b) For each schema-drift error, decide: is the property missing from the schema, or is the code wrong? Both are dangerous — pick a side per case. (c) Fix the `null`-narrowing errors with proper null guards. (d) Once clean, add a `tsc --noEmit` gate to a pre-commit hook (we already have husky). (e) Run `pnpm tsc --noEmit` in CI on every push.
**Est. effort:** 4–6 hours

### P0-4 · `server/agents/masterAgent.ts` — 3,300-line AI orchestrator, no tests
**File(s):** `server/agents/masterAgent.ts`
**LOC:** 3,300
**Severity reason:** This is the orchestrator for the entire AI tour-generation pipeline (WebScraperAgent → ContentAnalyzer → ColorTheme + ImagePrompt → ImageGen + Itinerary + Cost + Notice + Hotel + Meal + Flight parallel fanout). It's the heart of the product. Zero Vitest coverage despite being the #1 example of "業務邏輯 (AI 生成)" in CLAUDE.md §七. At 3,300 LOC it is 11× the CLAUDE.md limit. The progress-tracking, error-handling, retry, and cache-invalidation logic are all mixed in with the orchestration flow. If this breaks silently (e.g., an agent fails partway, progress hangs at 67%), it's invisible to monitoring.
**Recommendation:** (a) Extract the per-agent invocation wrappers into `server/agents/_pipeline/{webScraper,contentAnalyzer,colorTheme,imagePrompt,imageGen,itinerary,cost,notice,hotel,meal,flight}.ts` — each becomes a single 80–150 line function with one job. (b) Keep masterAgent.ts as the supervisor that only sequences calls and handles errors. Target: <400 lines. (c) Add Vitest tests for the supervisor with mocked agents — cover success path, partial-failure recovery, total-failure, and the 120-second timeout target. (d) Add at minimum 1 smoke test per extracted agent wrapper.
**Est. effort:** 16–24 hours (highest unknown — depends how tangled the cross-agent state is)

### P0-5 · `server/agents/autonomous/*` — 15 files, 5,000+ LOC, 0 tests, schema drift
**File(s):** All of `server/agents/autonomous/` (agentTools.ts 482, opsActions.ts 446, opsAgent.ts 494, gmailPipeline.ts 567, inquiryAgent.ts 391, selfRetrospective.ts 358, agentChat.ts 320, agentReport.ts 301, accountingAgent.ts 281, opsAgentStream.ts 238, reviewAgent.ts 182, refundAgent.ts 173, marketingAgent.ts 139, followupAgent.ts 122, officeAssistant.ts 83)
**LOC:** ~4,577 total across 15 files; 5 exceed 300 LOC
**Severity reason:** This subtree drives Jeff's Round 81 autonomous-agent vision. It currently has: (a) **zero Vitest coverage** for any of the 15 files, (b) **schema-drift tsc errors** in 4 of them (agentTools, opsActions, opsAgent, selfRetrospective), (c) `selfRetrospective.ts:219-224` has 4 implicit-any errors meaning the retrospective loop is silently typed-wrong, (d) `opsAgent.ts:411` calls `.content` on `InvokeResult` which the LLM core no longer returns. These agents take **autonomous actions on real customer data** (emails, refunds, bookings) — running them with type drift is a foot-gun. Most files are also unreviewed against CLAUDE.md size limits (5 files >300 LOC).
**Recommendation:** (a) Fix all autonomous-agent tsc errors **before** any further feature work. (b) Add a `agents/autonomous/__tests__/` directory and require minimum one `.test.ts` per agent (input→output, mocked LLM). (c) Split the 5 files >300 LOC: `agentTools.ts` → `agentTools/{bookings,tours,customers,gmail,index}.ts`; `opsAgent.ts` → policy + executor; `gmailPipeline.ts` → stages; `inquiryAgent.ts` → classifier + responder + index; `selfRetrospective.ts` → analyzer + reporter. (d) Audit each agent's "what real-world side effect does this have?" and add idempotency guards where the LLM could re-trigger an action.
**Est. effort:** 20–30 hours

## P1 — High-Priority

### P1-1 · `server/routers/agentRouter.ts` — 2,804 lines, 50 procedures, no tests
**File(s):** `server/routers/agentRouter.ts`
**LOC:** 2,804
**Severity reason:** This is the "split-out" router for autonomous agents — exactly the file pattern P0-1 is trying to promote — but it's already 9× past the 300-line limit. 50 procedures all admin-protected, covering customer-profile reads, interaction logging, policy versioning, agent metrics, Gmail integration, and 5 separate `demo*` procedures. Zero tests.
**Recommendation:** Split by top-level concern: `agentRouter/{profiles,interactions,outcomes,policies,metrics,messages,gmail,office,demos,opsActions,index}.ts`. Target ≤300 lines each. Add at least one happy-path test per file.
**Est. effort:** 8–10 hours

### P1-2 · `client/src/i18n/{zh-TW,en}.ts` — 6,200-line translation dictionaries
**File(s):** `client/src/i18n/zh-TW.ts` (6,220), `client/src/i18n/en.ts` (6,218)
**LOC:** 12,438 total
**Severity reason:** Single-key flat dictionaries that have grown without organization. Risk: when adding a new feature, AI agents can't easily find existing keys → duplicates accumulate → hard to keep zh/en in sync. There's already an `i18n-completeness.test.ts` which is good, but the structural problem persists. Also: 116 hard-coded Chinese strings still exist in JSX across the client (sample violations: `PhotoUploadSection.tsx:190`, `AuditLogTab.tsx:263-266`) — these should be moved into the i18n files, which makes the dictionaries grow even more.
**Recommendation:** Restructure both files into namespaced sub-modules: `i18n/zh-TW/{admin,booking,tours,membership,visa,common,...}.ts`, then a `i18n/zh-TW/index.ts` that re-exports a flat object so callers don't change. Same for en. Target ≤500 lines per sub-module. Tackle the 116 hard-coded Chinese strings as a second sub-task (separate progress.md entry).
**Est. effort:** 6–8 hours for restructure; +4–6 hours to migrate the 116 hard-coded strings

### P1-3 · `client/src/pages/TourDetailPeony.tsx` — 3,827-line page component
**File(s):** `client/src/pages/TourDetailPeony.tsx`
**LOC:** 3,827
**Severity reason:** Customer-facing tour detail page; 13× the limit. Contains the full daily itinerary rendering, photo gallery, booking flow integration, share/print logic. Highest-traffic page in the app. Helper tests exist (`server/TourDetailPeony.helpers.test.ts`) but the component itself is monolithic. If any single render block breaks (e.g., a malformed daily itinerary), the entire tour page fails.
**Recommendation:** Extract into `pages/TourDetailPeony/{HeroSection,DailyItinerary,Includes,Hotels,Meals,Flights,Departures,Booking,SimilarTours,index}.tsx`. Most of these sub-components already exist in `client/src/components/tour-detail/*` — the work is mainly moving inline JSX into those existing files and wiring props. Target index.tsx ≤300 lines.
**Est. effort:** 10–14 hours

### P1-4 · `server/db.ts` — 3,474-line query layer
**File(s):** `server/db.ts`
**LOC:** 3,474
**Severity reason:** All Drizzle queries in one file. Per CLAUDE.md §3.2 this is where DB access lives — but at 3,474 lines it's effectively unscannable. Called from every router and agent. The active tsc error `routers.ts:2453: Property 'listTours' does not exist on type 'typeof db'` likely means there's drift between what callers expect and what db.ts exports.
**Recommendation:** Split by domain: `server/db/{tours,bookings,users,payments,visa,inquiries,membership,packpoint,agents,...}.ts` + a `server/db/index.ts` that re-exports for backward compatibility. Document the public interface (which functions are stable vs. internal). Add unit tests for any function that does non-trivial SQL composition.
**Est. effort:** 8–12 hours

**File(s):**
- `server/agents/calibrationAgent.ts` (1,316; has 3 tsc errors)
- `server/email.ts` (1,302; no tests)
- `server/translation.ts` (1,269; HAS tests — keep)
- `client/src/pages/TourPrintView.tsx` (1,194)
- `client/src/components/admin/ToursTab.tsx` (1,149; has 1 tsc error)
- `client/src/components/admin/OfficeOverviewTab.tsx` (1,106)
- `client/src/pages/Tours.tsx` (1,103)
- `client/src/components/admin/AutonomousAgentsTab.tsx` (2,078; has 2 tsc errors)
- `client/src/components/admin/TourEditDialog.tsx` (2,156)
- `client/src/components/tour-detail/TourRouteMapCanvas.tsx` (1,882)
- `client/src/pages/ComponentShowcase.tsx` (1,436)
**LOC:** 11 files, all >1,000 LOC
**Severity reason:** Each individually fails CLAUDE.md §九. As a group they form the "next tier" of god-files after routers.ts/db.ts/masterAgent. Most have zero tests.
**Recommendation:** Treat as one P1 epic. For each, the recommended split is: (a) extract pure helper functions into adjacent `*.helpers.ts`, (b) extract sub-views into adjacent component files, (c) keep the entry file as orchestration only, target ≤400 lines. Each split gets one Vitest file covering the helpers.
**Est. effort:** 4–6 hours each × 11 = 44–66 hours total. Prioritize ToursTab + AutonomousAgentsTab (have tsc errors) and TourPrintView (customer-facing) first.

### P1-6 · `server/agents/skills/tourComparison.ts` — 650 LOC, just-shipped, no tests
**File(s):** `server/agents/skills/tourComparison.ts`
**LOC:** 650
**Severity reason:** Shipped in commit `0d8c658` (one commit before audit) with zero Vitest coverage. The file header explicitly says "this file is INTENTIONALLY the long, opinionated one" but at 650 LOC it's still 2× the CLAUDE.md limit. Owns business policy (bucket definitions, peak-window calendars, reject patterns, default regions per country) that **needs tests to prevent silent regressions** when policy changes. Calls `invokeLLM` for translation — already correct (server-side), but no test covers the cache-hit path.
**Recommendation:** (a) Extract policy data (BUCKETS, PEAK_WINDOWS, REJECT_PATTERNS, DEFAULT_REGIONS) into a `tourComparison.policy.ts` data-only file. (b) Extract the orchestration into `tourComparison.orchestrator.ts`. (c) Keep `tourComparison.ts` as the entry/index. (d) Add Vitest covering: bucket selection happy path, peak-window calendar lookup, reject pattern matching, fallback to default region. Target ≤300 lines each.
**Est. effort:** 4–6 hours

### P1-7 · Dead code: `FloatingOpsAgent.tsx` (retired this session)
**File(s):** `client/src/components/admin/FloatingOpsAgent.tsx`
**LOC:** ~unknown but no longer mounted (verified: only references are in comments and self-export)
**Severity reason:** Commit `4ef11ca` explicitly says "FloatingOpsAgent retired" and "FloatingOpsAgent.tsx file stays in the repo until next cleanup". Now is "next cleanup". Leaving deprecated UI components in the tree confuses AI agents and bloats the surface area future refactors have to consider. Also `TodayOverview.tsx` is wired only to a `today-legacy` fallback PageId — that page id should be removed too.
**Recommendation:** Delete `FloatingOpsAgent.tsx`. For `TodayOverview.tsx`: confirm with Jeff that the Round 81 UnifiedInbox rollout is complete; if yes, delete `TodayOverview.tsx` and remove the `today-legacy` PageId case from `pages/Admin.tsx`. Remove all stale comments that reference these components.
**Est. effort:** 1 hour

### P1-8 · `scripts/` graveyard — 102 files, many are anti-patterns
**File(s):** `/scripts/*` (102 files)
**LOC:** N/A
**Severity reason:** 102 one-off .mjs/.ts files including: 11 `fix-*` files (Taiwan tour fixes, color theme, train name, flights, category, router, tour data — all band-aids), 5 `apply-migration-*` files (migrations belong in `drizzle/`, not `scripts/`), 14 round-specific files (r67-*, round32, round63, round81, japan-sept-* — tied to dev rounds, should not be permanent), 18 `check-*` / `inspect-*` files (one-off diagnostics that should be a `/diagnose` skill), 4 retranslate-* (recurring task — should be a skill or admin route), `wipe-all-tours.mjs` (dangerous, no auth, no guard), `set-admin-password.mjs` (security-sensitive, ad-hoc). The `japan-sept-5-scrape.mjs` is the canonical anti-pattern Jeff already identified in memory.
**Recommendation:** Split into 5 buckets and act per bucket:
- **Promote to skill** (recurring, multi-use): `i18n-audit`, `mobile-audit`, `tour-detail-audit`, `inspect-recent-tours`, `retranslate-all-tours`, `llm_dashboard`, `sample-japan-products` → become server-side admin-callable skills under `server/services/skills/` or admin tRPC procedures.
- **Move to drizzle/migrations**: `apply-migration-0033/0034/0035`, `migrate-round32`, `migrate.mjs` — these should already be in `drizzle/migrations/` (audit confirmed `drizzle/migrations/` is empty at the repo root — migrations may live elsewhere; verify and consolidate).
- **Archive** (one-off historical, never re-run): all `fix-taiwan-*`, all `r67-*`, all `round63/round32` files, `japan-sept-*` → move to `scripts/_archive/2026-Q1/` and delete from PATH. Keep for git-history reference only.
- **Delete** (dangerous + redundant): `wipe-all-tours.mjs`, `quarantine-broken-japan.mjs`, `cleanup-test-data.mjs`, `purge-inactive-tours.mjs` — these are nukes without auth/dry-run. Replace with admin-procedure equivalents (already partly exist in routers).
- **Keep** (genuinely useful CLI tools): `check-admin.mjs`, `set-admin-password.mjs` (lock down with confirmation prompt), `pause-queue.mjs`, `verify-anthropic-credit.mjs`.
**Est. effort:** 6–8 hours total (mostly mechanical archival + 2-3 skill promotions)

### P1-9 · Hard-coded Chinese strings in JSX (116 instances)
**File(s):** 116 violations across `client/src/components/**` and `client/src/pages/**`. Sample: `PhotoUploadSection.tsx:190`, `AuditLogTab.tsx:263-266`.
**LOC:** N/A (116 sites)
**Severity reason:** Direct CLAUDE.md §4.1 violation: "禁止：硬編碼中文字串". The English version of the site renders Chinese in these spots, which is a customer-facing i18n bug. Pattern persists across newer admin tabs especially.
**Recommendation:** Sweep `grep -rn -E ">[一-龥]+<"` and replace each with `t('key')`. Add the corresponding key to both `zh-TW.ts` and `en.ts`. The structural i18n split (P1-2) should land first so we don't bloat the flat dictionaries further. After cleanup, add a lint rule (or a Vitest test like `i18n-completeness.test.ts`) that fails if Chinese characters appear in JSX text content outside the i18n dictionary itself.
**Est. effort:** 4–6 hours after P1-2 lands

### P1-10 · `server/services/supplierSyncService.ts` — type errors + no tests, financial-ish path
**File(s):** `server/services/supplierSyncService.ts`
**LOC:** 810
**Severity reason:** Phase 1C of the supplier-sync feature (Lion + UV catalog mirror) — this is the data backbone for the catalog the customer sees. Active tsc errors on lines 224 and 511 (`string` not assignable to `Date`). 2.7× the CLAUDE.md limit. No tests. If the sync writes wrong dates into the catalog mirror, the customer sees wrong departure dates → wrong prices → real refund liability.
**Recommendation:** Fix both type errors first (verify whether the source string is ISO-8601 and parse with `new Date(...)` or fix the column type). Add Vitest covering: (a) successful sync of a known supplier payload, (b) malformed-payload rejection, (c) date-string parsing. Split into `supplierSync/{lion,uv,index}.ts`.
**Est. effort:** 6–8 hours

---

## Refactor Sequence Recommendation

Suggested order, optimized for compounding safety (each step makes the next safer):

1. **Fix tsconfig + the 40 tsc errors (P0-3)** — Cheap, unblocks the "tsc must pass" rule, exposes hidden schema drift. Do this in **one focused session, fresh context**. Target: 4–6 hours.
2. **Delete dead code (P1-7)** — Reduces surface area before any other refactor touches surrounding files. Target: 1 hour.
3. **Stripe webhook tests + transactions (P0-2)** — Money path is the most expensive thing to break. Adding tests + transactions here BEFORE splitting routers.ts protects revenue. Target: 8–10 hours.
4. **Split `server/db.ts` (P1-4)** — Has to come before routers.ts split so the router sub-files can import only the domain they need. Target: 8–12 hours.
5. **Split `server/routers.ts` (P0-1)** — The biggest unlock. After db.ts is split this is mostly mechanical. Each sub-router gets one Vitest file. Target: 12–16 hours.
6. **i18n restructure + hard-coded Chinese cleanup (P1-2 + P1-9)** — Best done as one stage since they touch the same dictionary files. Target: 10–14 hours combined.
7. **Split autonomous agents (P0-5)** — Once tsc is clean (P0-3 done) and the supporting infrastructure is sane, attack the 15-file subtree. Target: 20–30 hours.
8. **Split masterAgent.ts (P0-4)** — The hardest one, save for last because the dependency graph is tangled. Target: 16–24 hours.
9. **Split remaining 1000+ LOC files (P1-5)** — Iterate through, one file per Vibe Coding cycle. Target: 44–66 hours.
10. **Scripts/ purge (P1-8)** — Mechanical archival; can interleave between heavier refactors as palate-cleansers. Target: 6–8 hours.

Total refactor budget: **~130–195 hours**, realistically 4–6 weeks of focused work with Vibe Coding discipline.

---

## Anti-pattern Inventory (one-off scripts that should be skills)

`/scripts/` contains 102 files. Categorized by what should happen to each:

### Should become server-side skills (admin-callable, reusable)
- `i18n-audit.mjs` → `server/services/skills/i18nAudit.ts`
- `mobile-audit.mjs` → `server/services/skills/mobileAudit.ts`
- `tour-detail-audit.mjs` → `server/services/skills/tourDetailAudit.ts`
- `inspect-recent-tours.mjs`, `tour-state-summary.mjs` → consolidate into `server/services/skills/tourInspector.ts`
- `llm_dashboard.mjs` → already partially in admin tab, finish wiring
- `retranslate-all-tours.mjs`, `retranslate-tours.mjs`, `clear-and-retranslate.mjs` → `server/services/skills/retranslateTours.ts` (admin procedure, one entry point)
- `compress-images.mjs`, `add-feature-images.mjs`, `add-itinerary-images.mjs` → consolidate into image-ops admin skill
- `verify-anthropic-credit.mjs` → admin dashboard tile

### Should be in `drizzle/migrations/` (not `scripts/`)
- `apply-migration-0033/0034/0035.mjs`
- `migrate-round32.mjs`, `migrate.mjs`

Note: `drizzle/migrations/` was found EMPTY at repo root during this audit. Migrations may live in another location (Drizzle config-defined) — verify and consolidate.

### Should be archived (historical, do-not-re-run)
- All `fix-taiwan-*` (4 files), all `r67-*` (3 files), all `round63/round32` (3 files), all `japan-sept-*` (3 files), `regen-2340001.mjs`, `trigger-test-batch.mjs`, `trigger-r67.mjs` → `scripts/_archive/2026-Q1/`

### Should be deleted (dangerous + replaced by admin procedures)
- `wipe-all-tours.mjs` (no auth)
- `quarantine-broken-japan.mjs` (one-off)
- `cleanup-test-data.mjs`, `cleanup-tours.mjs`, `purge-inactive-tours.mjs` (overlap with admin/routes)

### Keep but lock down
- `set-admin-password.mjs` — add confirmation prompt
- `check-admin.mjs`, `pause-queue.mjs` — keep as CLI tools

## Test Coverage Gaps (P0/P1 only)

The following high-risk files handle money / auth / autonomous-agent actions and have **zero Vitest coverage**:

| File | LOC | Risk |
|---|---|---|
| `server/_core/stripeWebhook.ts` | 1,019 | **Money** — Stripe events for bookings, visa, subscriptions, refunds |
| `server/routers.ts` | 10,122 | All 293 procedures including payment, booking, admin |
| `server/db.ts` | 3,474 | Every Drizzle query in the app |
| `server/agents/masterAgent.ts` | 3,300 | AI tour-generation orchestrator |
| `server/routers/agentRouter.ts` | 2,804 | Autonomous-agent admin endpoints |
| `server/email.ts` | 1,302 | Booking confirmations, refund notices |
| `server/queue.ts` | 892 | BullMQ workers |
| `server/agents/skills/tourComparison.ts` | 650 | Just-shipped business policy |
| `server/routers/plaidRouter.ts` | 820 | Banking integration |
| `server/services/supplierSyncService.ts` | 810 | Catalog data mirror |
| `server/_core/index.ts` | 924 | Server bootstrap / auth |
| `server/agents/autonomous/*` (15 files) | ~4,577 | Autonomous customer-facing actions (refunds, replies, follow-ups) |
| `server/marketingWorker.ts` | unknown | Marketing automation (has 1 tsc error) |
| `server/agents/skills/*.SKILL.md` | N/A | Documentation files OK, but the agents they describe lack tests |

**Recommendation:** Before any of these is refactored, add at minimum ONE happy-path Vitest test as a regression anchor. Use mocked I/O (db, LLM, Stripe). Stage 2 design.md should explicitly list the test stubs needed before the refactor itself starts.

---

## Statistics

- Total source files scanned: **507** (`.ts` + `.tsx` under `server/` and `client/src/`)
- Files >500 LOC: **75**
- Files >1,000 LOC: **19**
  - `server/routers.ts` (10,122)
  - `client/src/i18n/zh-TW.ts` (6,220)
  - `client/src/i18n/en.ts` (6,218)

  - `server/routers/agentRouter.ts` (2,804)
  - `client/src/components/admin/TourEditDialog.tsx` (2,156)

- Files with zero tests: ~459 out of 507 (only 48 `.test.ts` files exist, and most are co-located with simple helpers — not the high-risk files listed above)
- Files with existing tsc errors: **16** (40 errors total)
- Dead/orphaned files: **1 confirmed** (`FloatingOpsAgent.tsx`); **1 deprecation-pending** (`TodayOverview.tsx`)
- `scripts/` files: **102** (estimated ~70% should be archived/promoted/deleted)
- Database transactions used in production code: **5 call sites** (insufficient given the volume of multi-write paths)

---

## Notes on Scope

**Out of scope (handled elsewhere):**
- Security vulnerabilities below code-pattern level (covered in red-team rounds 1–7 + `docs/SECURITY_AUDIT_2026_05_14.md`)
- Dependency versions / CVEs (separate audit)
- Bundle size / performance optimization (separate audit)
- E2E / Playwright coverage (separate testing-strategy initiative)

**Ambiguities encountered (documented, not blocking):**
- `drizzle/migrations/` was found empty at repo root — migrations may live in a Drizzle-config-defined location. Verify before consolidating `scripts/apply-migration-*` files.
- Several "TodayOverview" comments suggest a staged rollout — confirm with Jeff that the legacy fallback path can be removed.
- `server/skills/` (newer, used by masterAgent) vs `server/services/skills/` (PDF/marketing skills) vs `server/agents/skills/` (skill docs) — three sibling locations all called "skills". Consolidating naming is P2 (not in this audit) but worth flagging for the next pass.
